text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
I head to the track for training only to<n>find a large
group of people already<n>there.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Curious, I make my way through the<n>crowd, only
to find...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Behold! Just by having this mystical<n>stone in your
possession, your luck<n>will increase!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
This here is a wooden bear carving<n>that improves
disposition with a<n>single touch! And this is a noh
mask<n>that grants you the power of flight!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
I'm here to share my blessings with<n>you all, a
parcel of fortune from me to<n>you! Please, give
them a try!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Matikanefukukitaru is talking up a big<n>show and
hawking her lucky wares<n>to all the Umamusume
passing by.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Why, hello there, Trainer! Feel free to<n>try one out
yourself!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text: I start with the mask... but nothing<n>happens.
choice_data_list: My feet are still on the ground.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Well then! Don't you worry, there is<n>still plenty of
good fortune to go<n>around!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
I mean, I just can't keep myself from<n>winning!
Left or right, here or there, all<n>I find is fortune at
every turn!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Doesn't that make me the luckiest<n>Umamusume
around? Surely, it does!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
She's won the Kyoto Shimbun Hai,<n>Kobe Shimbun
Hai, and Kikuka Sho,<n>among others. Luck certainly
has<n>been on her side. However...
choice_data_list: (I think she's getting way too carried away...)

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
She's been acting strange ever since<n>the Kikuka
Sho. Her attitude is<n>worrisome, to say the least.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text: No sooner do I have that thought,<n>when...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: How do you do, Matikanefukukitaru?

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I'm pleased to find you in good<n>spirits. A beautiful
autumn day, is it<n>not?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
It's good to see you too, Bright! What<n>auspicious
timing!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
We see each other quite often, but<n>I've been
feeling a particularly<n>special connection between
us lately.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Maybe it's because we've been<n>competing in so
many of the same<n>races! I have just the thing for
you!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Oh my, is that so? Which of these<n>goods are you
referring to?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: This! Behold, the Great God of Fish<n>Cakes!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Hehehe! It's exactly what it sounds<n>like—a figure
of a god carved out of a<n>board for serving fish
cakes!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
It represents a fleet-footed deity.<n>Carry it around,
and you might carve<n>a few seconds off your best
time!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Goodness! What a wonderful gift. I'm<n>ever so
grateful.

Mejiro Bright takes the slightly<n>misshapen Great
God of Fish Cakes.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Hehe. I'll be sure to carry it with me<n>every day to
reap its benefits.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Yes, please do! I'm sure you'll find it<n>to be
stupendously effective!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
If not, I fear I'll never be capable of<n>winning
against you,<n>Matikanefukukitaru.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: ...Huh?

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I may have suffered defeat at your<n>hands on a
number of occasions, but<n>that is all in the past
now.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I am of the firm belief that true victory<n>comes only
after a series of bitter<n>defeats.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Soon enough, victory will come within<n>my reach—a
truly brilliant victory.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Oh... Would you look at the time? I<n>must be off to
my training.

Until we meet again, on the day I<n>emerge
victorious.

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Beneath her serene smile, Mejiro<n>Bright simmers
with fierce<n>determination. As for
Matikanefukukitaru...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: I can't believe it.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: She will emerge victorious? Againstthe luckiest Umamusume on theplanet?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
That's quite the declaration! But she<n>forgets I am
protected by the gods!<n>For I possess... the Fish
Cake God of<n>War!

text_block_list.name: Monólogo
text_block_list.text:
Mejiro Bright's provocation is lost on
Matikanefukukitaru, who's off in her<n>own
universe. If anything, it only<n>makes her more
confident.
